title: prunus padus monstr. aucubifolia
intro: Prunus padus monstr. aucubifolia, also known as the Monstera-leafed Bird Cherry or more commonly as the  Bird Cherry (Prunus padus), is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant contains flavonoids, alkaloids, and other compounds with potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Prunus padus include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. Prunus padus is typically prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal uses, though more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential interactions.
